channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigerian Army has commenced the basic military training of 5,300 newly recruited soldiers following their formal handover to The post Army begins training of 5,300 newly recruited soldiers in Osogbo appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Choosing Life Partner Goes Beyond Virginity – Chiwetalu Agu
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actor Chiwetalu Agu has urged Nigerian men not to choose partners based on virginity or physical qualities. The actor who shared his opinion on relationships and marriage, said while virginity may represent purity, it does not represent loyalty, good character, emotional intelligen

item.title: Army Intercepts Truck Loaded With Suspected Illicit Drugs, Arrests Driver In Lagos
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Troops of the 65 Battalion of the Nigerian Army have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of suspected illicit drugs along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road and arrested the driver. The operation was carried out on July 9, 2026, following actionable intelligence received from a credible so

item.title: ACF, MBF, NOSCEF, others react to APC’s 2027 Muslim-Muslim presidential ticket
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: ACF, MBF, NOSCEF and other stakeholders react to APC’s decision to retain the Tinubu-Shettima Muslim-Muslim ticket for 2027. The post ACF, MBF, NOSCEF, others react to APC’s 2027 Muslim-Muslim presidential ticket appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Youths protest at Och’Idoma’s palace, block roads with corpses, demand action By Peter Duru, Makurdi At least 18 persons have been killed in separate attacks on communities in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State, following fresh incursions by suspected armed herdsmen. The attacks, which occ

item.title: Tinubu govt struggling with failure in security, economy, health, education – ADC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Democratic Congress, ADC, on Sunday said President Bola Tinubu’s administration is struggling under the weight of its failures. ADC’s National Publicity Secretary, Bolaji Abdullahi, said the Tinubu administration has failed in the areas of security, economy, healthcare, educa

item.title: Anambra Police Arrest 3 Over N2m Child Trafficking
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Anambra State Police Command has arrested three female suspects over the sale of two children at the sum of Two million at desperate occasions in 2025. In a statement by the Police Public Relations Officer SP Tochukwu Ikenga narrated that the three suspects have been helping police with vital inform

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator Barau Jibrin says state police will strengthen security, improve local policing and address insecurity with constitutional safeguards. The post State police: President’s masterstroke against insecurity — Deputy Senate President appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: 16 feared dead following suspected herdsmen attack in Benue community
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: At least 16 people have been killed in a fresh attack by suspected armed herders on Nobi community in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State, triggering widespread protests and renewed concerns over insecurity in the state. The post 16 feared dead following suspected herdsmen attack in Benue co

item.title: Car dealers express mixed reactions over lower vehicle import duties
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: They said that the policy would reduce vehicle prices, lower business costs and benefit buyers, but noted that its overall impact would depend on other economic factors, including exchange rates. item.title: Why I can never collaborate with some of my colleagues – Kiekie

Speaking in a recent podcast interview, she stated that she can never partner with any skit makers that are too vulgar and

item.title: Police Confirm Market Woman Died While Returning Home
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Cross River State Police Command has confirmed the death of a market women who gave up after the day’s business and was heading home. The command’s Public Relations Officér, ASP Eitokpa Sunday Akata made the confirmation on Sunday in a press statement to clarify what many people are alle

item.title: How Philips Hue got the smart home right
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The state of the smart home can be frustrating, because it is just so obvious how things ought to work. You should be able to control everything from everywhere. Your spaces should adapt to what you're doing and how you're feeling. Making your home smart shouldn't require renovating, and the smarts

item.title: Kogi bans roadside revenue collection, haulage fees
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Enehe said the new tax regime expressly prohibited unauthorised revenue collection on highways carried out under the guise of taxation. The post Kogi bans roadside revenue collection, haulage fees appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Social media activist, Martins Vincent Otse, popularly known as VeryDarkMan, VDM, has claimed that some Fulani people sent him a video clip that probably triggered the recent deadly attack in Otukpo, Benue State. Kalu in a statement on Sunday described the decision as a bold step

item.dc:creator: The Sun

She says Phia has acknowledged and fix

item.title: UniAbuja promotes 34 academics to professorial ranks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Joseph Erunke, Abuja The Governing Council of the University of Abuja, now Yakubu Gowon University, has approved the promotion of 34 academic staff to professorial ranks. The approval was granted under the leadership of the Council Chairman, Distinguished Senator Dr Olanrewaju Tejuoso. Seventeen

item.title: Calabar flood: Parents mourn as landslide kills four children
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A rainfall-induced landslide killed four siblings and one adult in Calabar, prompting calls for a geotechnical assessment of the affected area. The post Calabar flood: Parents mourn as landslide kills four children appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Government has approved the Reviewed Scheme of Service for the Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet), marking a landmark and progressive move. The move, it was learnt, would further amplify momentum to the ongoing rebirth and secure fair wages, with the overall aim of empowering Nigeria

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Akwa Ibom State governor, Umo Eno, has assured victims of recent floods that ravaged most parts of Uyo and the adjoining Itam, in Itu local government area of support as the state launches emergency operations. He consoled families and individuals affected by the flash-flood and called for concerted

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman for allegedly attempting to smuggle 5.75 kilograms of heroin into Nigeria and seized illicit drugs worth more than ₦10.3 billion in separate operations across the country. The suspect, identified as Ms. J

The decisive move follows the Governor’

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Donald Trump paid tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".

item.title: Lagos Orders Clinics To Conduct Malaria Test Before Treating Fever
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Lagos State Commissioner for Health, Prof Akin Abayomi, has called on hospitals, clinics and drug shops to stop guessing and start testing patients properly before giving malaria drugs, saying only about five per cent of fever cases in Lagos are caused by malaria. Abayomi announced this new rule for

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has confirmed that world football’s governing body will examine a proposal to expand the FIFA World Cup to 64 teams for the 2030 tournament. The move would represent another major change to football’s biggest international competition, which has already expanded from

item.description: Solar energy systems are a good alternative to help Yemenis access electricity but there are hidden dangers.

item.title: UK police says no evidence of political motive in murder ​of former minister Widdecombe
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Police on Sunday said that there is no evidence that the suspected murder ​of former British government minister Ann Widdecombe was politically motivated. Widdecombe, 78, was found dead at ⁠her home in rural southwest England on Thursday with what police described as "serious injuries".

item.description: Nigerian businesswoman Okoye Chidinma has sparked reactions after stating that she would never marry a man who earns less than she does, saying she would struggle to respect such a partner. item.title: Best performing Equity Mutual Funds in Nigeria as of June
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria's equity mutual fund segment contracted to a net asset value (NAV) of N234.86 billion as of June 26, 2026, down 11.98% from N266.82. The post Best performing Equity Mutual Funds in Nigeria as of June appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: PPPs best option to bridge West Africa’s infrastructure gap — ICRC DG
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Emma Ujah, Abuja Bureau Chief The Director-General of the Infrastructure Concession Regulatory Commission (ICRC), Dr. Jobson Oseodion Ewalefoh, has urged West African countries to adopt Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs) as a key strategy for addressing the region’s infrastructure deficit.

item.title: The Sundiata Post Model (2): Historical and intellectual foundations of a knowledge-producing newsroom, By Max Amuchie
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Every institutional framework has a history, even when it introduces something new. item.title: Man In Police Net For Staging Own Abduction In Ondo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a 33-year-old man, Olawale Sikiru, for allegedly staging his own abduction and collecting ransom from his family in a desperate bid for financial gain. The police also arrested a 23-year-old suspect, Agbelusi Ayomide, for allegedly conspiring with Sikiru an

Today, I don’t just celebrate my age, I celebrate my growth, my survival, my wins, the quiet strength that carried me through eve

item.title: The $360 Billion Market that Medicine Got Wrong for 20 Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The $360 billion dollar women’s health market is made of several categories that are often overlooked – one of which is menopause. The condition has gone largely unnoticed by pharma and biotech firms, employers, and investors, despite affecting half the world’s population and contributing to major l

item.description: If you're reading an Oura Ring 5 review at The Verge, you likely fall into one of two camps: newcomers looking for a smartwatch alternative, or Oura users pondering an upgrade. item.title: Earnings estimates have been following an unusual pattern this time around
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Analysts generally lower their estimates in the months before earnings. But thanks to the energy and tech sectors, expectations have actually climbed heading into second-quarter results.

item.title: Atiku: WAEC, NECO fee hike may send many children out of school
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has described the recent increase in fees for Federal Unity Colleges and the approval of a uniform ₦50,000 examination fee for WAEC and NECO candidates from 2027 as cruel and economically insensitive. item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Here is why Senegal's head coach Pape Thiaw was sacked by the Senegalese football association after the 2026 World Cup exit. The post Why Senegal sacked Pape Thiaw appeared first on NotjustOk.

item.title: Fresh Strikes Threaten US Iran Talks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar and ABC News' Shannon Kingston say renewed attacks in and around the Strait of Hormuz have undermined fragile diplomatic efforts, with Gulf allies pressing to revive negotiations as shipping and regional security remain under threat. item.title: Court bars police lawyers not appointed as legal officers from prosecution
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The judge also ordered the police force, Police Service Commission (PSC) and the Inspector-General of the Federation to deploy “police officers who are legal practitioners to every Police Division in Nigeria to assist with the enforcement of human rights in policing at the Divisions.” The post Court

item.title: “Bandits Have No Religion, Don’t Call Them Muslims” — Oluwo of Iwo
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: “Bandits Have No Religion, The Oluwo of Iwo, Oba Abdulrosheed Akanbi, has strongly criticised the practice of describing bandits as Muslims, insisting that criminality should not be linked to Islam. In a statement released on Saturday through his Chief Press Secretary, Ibrahim Alli, the monarc

item.title: 2026 World Cup: FIFA releases statement on Golden Boot race between Mbappe, Messi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: FIFA has confirmed that France captain, Kylian Mbappe, is leading Argentina’s Lionel Messi in the race to win the Golden Boot at the 2026 World Cup. The world football governing body made the clarification in a statement on Sunday. Both Mbappe and Messi have scored the same number of goals (8)

item.title: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, local media reports.
